Title: API key exposed in /dt-config endpoint
Description: nginx returns the DT_API_KEY in a plain JSON response at /dt-config that any browser or caller reaching port 3000 can read without authentication.
Recommendation: Remove apiKey from the /dt-config JSON response. Users enter the key manually in the Connect modal.

Title: TLS certificate verification unconditionally disabled
Description: rejectUnauthorized: false in server.js disables all TLS chain validation for every outbound HTTPS call. A network-level MITM can intercept DT API traffic silently.
Recommendation: Make it opt-in via an env var (e.g. DT_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=false). Default should be true (verify certs).

Title: No request body size limit in readBody()
Description: A caller sending a multi-gigabyte payload to POST /violation-cache/config will exhaust process memory and crash the service.
Recommendation: Reject the request once accumulated bytes exceed ~64 KB and return HTTP 413.

Title: No rate limiting on /violation-cache/refresh
Description: Any browser tab or external caller can spam the refresh endpoint forcing repeated expensive full-violation fetches and exhausting CPU/IO.
Recommendation: Enforce a minimum cooldown between job starts (e.g. 60 s). Return HTTP 429 if a new request arrives within the cooldown window.

Title: .env file permissions not documented or enforced
Description: The .env file contains the DT API key. Default file creation permissions (644) leave it world-readable on the host OS.
Recommendation: Add chmod 600 .env to install.sh and document the requirement in INSTALLATION.md.

Title: No Content-Security-Policy header on dashboard
Description: Without a CSP header injected scripts (XSS) run without restriction in the browser.
Recommendation: Add a tight CSP to nginx.conf.template: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:

Title: Missing X-Frame-Options and X-Content-Type-Options headers
Description: Dashboard can be embedded in an iframe (clickjacking risk) and browsers may MIME-sniff responses.
Recommendation: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY and X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff headers to nginx.conf.template.

Title: No URL validation on user-provided API URL in Connect modal
Description: The browser Connect modal accepts any string as the API URL without validating it is a valid http/https URL before fetch() is called.
Recommendation: Validate with new URL(input) and reject non-http(s) schemes before using the value.

Title: Cache file written with default world-readable permissions
Description: violation-cache.json is created with system umask (typically 0644) making it world-readable on the host.
Recommendation: Pass { mode: 0o640 } to fs.writeFileSync when writing the cache and tmp files.

Title: Dashboard runs HTTP only with no TLS guidance
Description: nginx listens on HTTP port 80. API key and project data transit in plaintext between client and server if not behind a TLS proxy.
Recommendation: Document that the dashboard must sit behind an HTTPS-terminating reverse proxy. Optionally provide a sample TLS config block in nginx.conf.template.

Title: No HTTP keep-alive agent pooling in cache service
Description: Every dtGet() call may open a new TCP+TLS connection. With 9 parallel pipelines and hundreds of pages this creates hundreds of short-lived connections.
Recommendation: Create an https.Agent({ keepAlive: true maxSockets: 20 }) and share it across all requests.

Title: Page total inaccurate during early job progress
Description: jobProgress.pagesTotal is incremented as each of the 9 pipeline page-1 fetches completes. The running total shown in the browser is undercounted until all page-1 fetches finish.
Recommendation: Two-phase approach: run all 9 page-1 fetches first to discover total pages then start fetching remaining pages. Set pagesTotal once after discovery phase.

Title: Regex compiled per-project in inferParentUuids
Description: new RegExp(...) is called inside the loop for every project that has a version string. For portfolios with thousands of projects this is measurably slow.
Recommendation: Pre-compile the regex once outside the loop.

Title: No text-search index for large portfolios
Description: applyFilters() walks the full allProjects array on every debounced keypress. For portfolios above ~10K projects filter latency becomes noticeable.
Recommendation: Build an inverted index of project name tokens on load for instant text lookup. Fall back to full scan only for non-text filter changes.

Title: CSV export builds one giant in-memory string
Description: filtered.map(...).join() creates the entire CSV in a single string. For 50K+ projects this can consume 50-200 MB of heap.
Recommendation: Assemble CSV from chunked Blobs or use a streaming WritableStream to avoid holding the full string in memory at once.

Title: No HTTP caching headers on /violation-cache/data
Description: The cache file changes at most once per TTL period but is served with Cache-Control: no-store forcing a full re-download on every page load.
Recommendation: Serve Cache-Control: private max-age=300 and ETag/Last-Modified headers so browsers can use a cached copy for up to 5 minutes.

Title: Docker image not optimised — no .dockerignore and base image unpinned
Description: No .dockerignore file exists so the full build context is sent to Docker on every build. node:18-alpine resolves to the latest patch at build time breaking reproducibility.
Recommendation: Add .dockerignore. Pin the base image to a specific digest: FROM node:18-alpine@sha256:<digest>.

Title: nginx gzip_types list missing text/html and application/json
Description: The current gzip_types list omits text/html and application/json. The dashboard's large HTML payload and JSON API responses are served uncompressed.
Recommendation: Add text/html and application/json to the gzip_types directive in nginx.conf.template.

Title: Zero automated tests
Description: No test suite exists for server.js (env parsing cache logic HTTP endpoints) or dashboard filter/sort/render logic. Any regression requires full manual verification.
Recommendation: Add unit tests using Node's built-in node:test runner (no npm needed) for server.js. Add Playwright smoke tests for the dashboard.

Title: Dashboard IIFE provides no real scope isolation
Description: ~15 state variables are declared inside an IIFE but ~30 functions are then explicitly re-exported to window. The IIFE scope provides no meaningful isolation — all state is accessible from the browser console.
Recommendation: Either remove the IIFE and use explicit globals or convert to <script type=module> and export only what inline event handlers need.

Title: No 'use strict' in dashboard JavaScript
Description: server.js has 'use strict' on line 1 but the dashboard IIFE does not. Sloppy mode allows accidental globals and silently ignores some class of errors.
Recommendation: Add 'use strict'; as the first statement inside the dashboard IIFE.

Title: Magic numbers not named throughout dashboard
Description: 200 (debounce ms) 500 (project page size) 5000 (poll interval ms) 3000 (probe retry ms) are scattered inline with no explanation.
Recommendation: Collect all tunable constants in a single CONFIG = { DEBOUNCE_MS: 200 PAGE_SIZE: 500 POLL_INTERVAL_MS: 5000 ... } block at the top of the script.

Title: Pill renderer coupled to literal level name strings
Description: pillFor() / pillClass() branch on literal strings ('critical' 'fail' etc.). Adding a new risk level or renaming one requires changes in multiple places.
Recommendation: Declare a LEVEL_STYLE map object; make pillFor a pure lookup into that map.

Title: Mock data is non-deterministic
Description: generateMockProjects() uses Math.random() seeded fresh on every page load. Mock data changes on every reload making visual regression testing impossible.
Recommendation: Add a simple seeded pseudo-random number generator (LCG — no dependencies) so mock data is deterministic and reproducible.

Title: patchEnvFile() breaks on Windows line endings
Description: patchEnvFile uses split('\n') to parse the .env file. If the file was created on Windows (CRLF line endings) trailing \r characters corrupt keys and values on write.
Recommendation:
Normalise line endings before splitting: content.replace(/
/g '
').split('
').

Title: patchEnvFile() write errors are unhandled
Description: patchEnvFile uses synchronous fs.writeFileSync with no try-catch. An EACCES or ENOSPC error propagates up and crashes the process via the unhandled-rejection handler.
Recommendation: Wrap fs.writeFileSync in patchEnvFile inside a try-catch; log the error and let the caller return HTTP 500.

Title: getStatus() reads and parses the full cache file on every status poll
Description: The browser polls /violation-cache/status every 5 s. Each poll calls readCacheFile() which does fs.readFileSync + JSON.parse on the full cache file (potentially megabytes).
Recommendation: Cache the metadata fields (generatedAt expiresAt projectCount failedPipelines) in an in-memory object after each job completes. Only update after a job finishes.

Title: Cache file is parsed twice on a single page load
Description: readCacheFile() is called in getStatus() and again when GET /violation-cache/data is requested moments later during the same page load.
Recommendation: Use in-memory metadata from Q9 for status; GET /violation-cache/data reads the raw file buffer directly (no JSON.parse) and pipes it to the response.

Title: No SPDX license identifier in source files
Description: server.js index.html install.sh and nginx.conf.template have no machine-readable SPDX header. OSS scanners (FOSSA REUSE scancode) flag these files as unlicensed.
Recommendation: Add // SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT (and Copyright line) to the top of each source file.

Title: No NOTICE or ATTRIBUTION file
Description: No attribution file exists for third-party components. Best practice for MIT/Apache projects.
Recommendation: Create a NOTICE file listing any third-party assets or inspirations.

Title: No SECURITY.md vulnerability disclosure policy
Description: GitHub displays a Security policy tab when SECURITY.md exists. Without it the repo shows No security policy — a reputational concern for a security-adjacent tool.
Recommendation: Add a minimal SECURITY.md with supported versions and a responsible disclosure process.

Title: Docker base image not pinned to a digest
Description: FROM node:18-alpine resolves to whatever the latest patch is at build time. This allows silent supply-chain changes and breaks reproducible builds.
Recommendation: Pin to a digest: FROM node:18-alpine@sha256:<digest> and document how to update it.

Title: Node.js 18 is end-of-life (April 2025)
Description: Node 18 LTS reached EOL in April 2025 and no longer receives security patches. Docker images built from node:18-alpine are unpatched.
Recommendation: Upgrade Dockerfile base image to node:22-alpine (current LTS).

Title: install.sh missing copyright and license header
Description: The script is a substantial standalone work. Without a header its license status is ambiguous to adopters and OSS scanners.
Recommendation: Add SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT and a copyright notice as a comment at the top of install.sh.

Title: No Docker health checks defined
Description: Docker cannot determine if either service is alive or stuck. docker-compose depends_on has no condition: service_healthy check so the dashboard may start before the cache service is ready.
Recommendation: Add healthcheck entries to both services in docker-compose.yml. Cache service: GET /violation-cache/status. Dashboard: GET /.

Title: No Docker log rotation configured
Description: Docker json-file log driver grows without bound. On a long-running server this will eventually fill the disk.
Recommendation: Add logging.driver: json-file with max-size: 100m and max-file: 5 options to both service definitions in docker-compose.yml.

Title: Plain-text logs not structured JSON
Description: All logs in server.js are console.log plain strings. Aggregating into Datadog Loki Grafana or similar requires fragile regex parsing.
Recommendation: Add a simple log(level message fields) helper that emits JSON ({ level info msg ... ts ... }) toggled by a LOG_FORMAT=json env var.

Title: Installer does not validate API key against DT
Description: install.sh prompts for the API key and writes it to .env without testing it. The user discovers a bad key only after containers start and the first cache build fails.
Recommendation: Add a curl test after key entry: GET /api/v1/project?pageSize=1 with the key and verify HTTP 200. Warn or abort on failure.

Title: No CHANGELOG.md
Description: No record of what changed between versions making it hard for adopters to track upgrades and breaking changes.
Recommendation: Maintain a CHANGELOG.md following Keep a Changelog format (https://keepachangelog.com).
